summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard of the Oct 25 1979 second set delivers a scorching Shakedown Street, soaring Passenger and blistering Eyes of the World.
review: The recording is a high‑fidelity soundboard capture – crisp, well balanced and with Brent’s funky keyboards front‑and‑center.  Musically the show is a nonstop blaze: an over‑16‑minute Shakedown Street launches into a powerhouse Passenger, then rolls through Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World with tight transitions, standout solos from Jerry and Phil, and a soulful Stella Blue to close.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive comments on both performance (many 4‑5 star reviews praising the jams) and technical quality (crisp soundboard, clear mix). Only a few minor complaints about missing first set or occasional muddiness.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clean mix with prominent Brent keyboards; occasional slight muddiness noted around the middle of Shakedown but no major dropouts. First set missing from this source.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A high‑energy second set that never lets up – Shakedown opens the marathon, segues into a monster Passenger, then flows through Estimated Prophet → Eyes with seamless jams and tight drum/space excursions before winding down on Stella Blue.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers rave about his lightning‑fast runs, "gatling gun" soloing on Passenger and Shakedown, and his emotive vocals on Eyes.
band_member_comments.Phil: His bass is highlighted as dominant on Estimated Prophet and throughout the set, providing a powerful backbone.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s funky clavinet/synth/organ work is repeatedly praised as a driving force, often described as "funky", "razed‑up" and even "Edgar Winter‑like".
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill and Mickey are called masterful, with the drum jam after Eyes noted as a highlight; a brief equipment repair moment is also mentioned.
